heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 118–31, div. E, title LVII, § 5702(d)(1)137 Stat. 960Dec. 22, 2023
label: 1801 Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 118–31, div. E, title LVII, § 5702(d)(1)137 Stat. 960Dec. 22, 2023
notes: Section, , , ; , , , related to appointment of Architect of the Capitol by the President by and with the advice and consent of the Senate for a term of 10 years. See .Pub. L. 101–163, title III, § 319103 Stat. 1068Pub. L. 104–19, title I, § 701109 Stat. 220section 1801a of this titleNov. 21, 1989July 27, 1995

CodificationSection was classified to section 162–1 of former Title 40, prior to the enactment of Title 40, Public Buildings, Property, and Works, by , , .Pub. L. 107–217, § 1116 Stat. 1062Aug. 21, 2002

Prior Provisions, , transferred duties relative to the Capitol theretofore performed by Commissioner of Public Buildings and Grounds to Architect of the Capitol.Act Aug. 15, 1876, ch. 28719 Stat. 147, , abolished office of Commissioner of Public Buildings and Grounds referred to in , and transferred the duties of that office to the Chief of Engineers of the Army.Act Mar. 2, 1867, ch. 167, § 214 Stat. 466section 1811 of this title, , made appropriation for “the extension of the Capitol” according to the plan as might be approved by the President, to be expended under his direction, “by such architect as he may appoint to execute the same.” Subsequent acts frequently referred to the Architect of the Capitol or to the Architect of the Capitol Extension.Act Sept. 30, 1850, ch. 90, § 19 Stat. 538, , authorized President to continue office of Architect of the Capitol long enough to complete work in progress.Act Mar. 3, 1829, ch. 51, § 24 Stat. 363, , abolished office of Architect of the Capitol. The duties of that office were transferred to Commissioner of Public Buildings and Grounds, appointed by President under , , to succeed a previously existing board of three commissioners of Public Buildings and Grounds.Act May 2, 1828, ch. 45, § 34 Stat. 266act April 29, 1816, ch. 150, § 23 Stat. 324

Change of NameThe name of Superintendent of the Capitol Building and Grounds was changed to Architect of the Capitol by , , the Legislative, Executive, and Judicial Appropriation Act , fiscal year 1922.Act Mar. 3, 1921, ch. 12441 Stat. 1291Mar. 3, 1921The name of Architect of the Capitol was changed to Superintendent of the Capitol Building and Grounds, by , , popularly known as the “Urgent Deficiency Appropriation Act for 1902”.act Feb. 14, 1902, ch. 1732 Stat. 20
Effective Date of RepealRepeal applicable with respect to appointments made on or after , see .Dec. 22, 2023section 1801a(e) of this title

Short Title of 2023 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 118–31, div. E, title LVII, § 5701137 Stat. 960Dec. 22, 2023“This title [enacting sections 1801a and 1805a of this title, amending sections 1805 and 1811 of this title, and repealing this section and ] may be cited as the ‘Architect of the Capitol Appointment Act of 2023’.”section 1804 of this title
Short Title of 2016 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–156, § 1130 Stat. 391May 16, 2016“This Act [enacting subchapter V of this chapter] may be cited as the ‘Fallen Heroes Flag Act of 2016’.”

Comprehensive Management Study and Response, , , required the Comptroller General to conduct a comprehensive management study of the operations of the Architect of the Capitol by , and required the Architect of the Capitol to develop and submit a management improvement plan.Pub. L. 107–68, title I, § 129(d)115 Stat. 580Nov. 12, 2001Nov. 1, 2002

Accounting and Financial Management System, , , which directed the Architect of the Capitol to develop and maintain an accounting and financial management system, including financial reporting and internal controls, was from the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 2002.Pub. L. 107–68, title I, § 132115 Stat. 581Nov. 12, 2001
